---
title: "Figure 1B"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ellipse)

# --- 1. Definir los parámetros (Matrices) ---
# Prior: Muy incierto (grande), correlación moderada
mu_prior <- c(0, 0)
cov_prior <- matrix(c(2.0, 1.0, 
                      1.0, 2.0), 2)

# Posterior: Más certero (pequeño), correlación fuerte, media desplazada
mu_post <- c(1, 1)
cov_post <- matrix(c(0.5, 0.4, 
                     0.4, 0.5), 2)

# --- 2. Generar datos geométricos (NO simulación) ---
# Función auxiliar para crear el data.frame
get_ellipse <- function(cov, mu, level, type) {
  df <- as.data.frame(ellipse(cov, centre = mu, level = level, npoints = 200))
  df$Type <- type
  return(df)
}

# Generamos contornos al 95% de probabilidad
df_prior <- get_ellipse(cov_prior, mu_prior, 0.95, "Prior (Creencia Inicial)")
df_post  <- get_ellipse(cov_post, mu_post, 0.95, "Posterior (Con Datos)")
df_all   <- rbind(df_prior, df_post)

# --- 3. Plotting estilo Publicación ---
ggplot(df_all, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = Type, color = Type)) +
  
  # A. Relleno semitransparente (Simula el "volumen" sin tapar)
  geom_polygon(alpha = 0.3) + 
  
  # B. Borde sólido (Define el límite matemático exacto)
  geom_path(linewidth = 1) +  
  
  # C. Puntos centrales (Medias)
  annotate("point", x = mu_prior[1], y = mu_prior[2], shape = 3, size = 3, color = "#777777") +
  annotate("point", x = mu_post[1], y = mu_post[2], shape = 19, size = 3, color = "#D55E00") +

  # D. Estética minimalista académica
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#999999", "#D55E00")) +  # Gris neutro vs Naranja fuerte
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#555555", "#D55E00")) +
  
  coord_fixed() + # Importante para no distorsionar la correlación
  theme_bw() +    # Fondo blanco limpio
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.15, 0.85),       # Leyenda dentro del plot (ahorra espacio)
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()     # Menos ruido visual
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Actualización Bayesiana",
    x = "Parámetro $\\theta_1$", # LaTeX notation si usas renderizadores
    y = "Parámetro $\\theta_2$"
  )
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ellipse)

# --- 1. Helper Function ---
# Generates a data frame for an ellipse given Mean and Covariance
get_ellipse_df <- function(mu, sigma, label, level = 0.95) {
  # Calculate ellipse points
  # ellipse() expects a correlation matrix or covariance matrix
  # If sigma is covariance, we pass it directly.
  coords <- ellipse(sigma, centre = mu, level = level, npoints = 200)
  df <- as.data.frame(coords)
  df$Type <- label # Tag the data for ggplot grouping
  return(df)
}

# --- 2. Define Parameters ---

# A. PRIOR (High uncertainty, centered at 0,0)
mu_prior <- c(0, 0)
sigma_prior <- matrix(c(2.0, 1.2,  # Large variance, some correlation
                        1.2, 2.0), nrow = 2)

# B. POSTERIOR (Lower uncertainty, shifted center)
# (In a real scenario, you calculate this via conjugate math. 
# Here we define it manually to show the visual effect.)
mu_post <- c(1.5, 1.0)
sigma_post <- matrix(c(0.8, 0.4,   # Tighter covariance
                       0.4, 0.6), nrow = 2)

# --- 3. Generate Data ---
df_prior <- get_ellipse_df(mu_prior, sigma_prior, "Prior")
df_post  <- get_ellipse_df(mu_post, sigma_post, "Posterior")
plot_data <- rbind(df_prior, df_post)

# Create a separate data frame for the means (centers)
centers <- data.frame(
  x = c(mu_prior[1], mu_post[1]),
  y = c(mu_prior[2], mu_post[2]),
  Type = c("Prior", "Posterior")
)

# --- 4. Plot with ggplot ---
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = Type, fill = Type)) +
  
  # A. The Ellipses (95% Confidence Regions)
  geom_polygon(alpha = 0.2) + # Filled region
  geom_path(linewidth = 1) +  # Outline
  
  # B. The Means (Center points)
  geom_point(data = centers, size = 3) +
  
  # C. Styling
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#E69F00", "Posterior" = "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#E69F00", "Posterior" = "#56B4E9")) +
  coord_fixed() + # Keeps the geometry true
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Bayesian Update: Prior vs. Posterior",
       subtitle = "95% Probability Regions",
       x = "Parameter 1",
       y = "Parameter 2")
```
